epics.title: Telegram Account Session Management
epics.jtbd: When scaling outreach campaigns, I want to securely add and manage Telegram accounts with dedicated proxies and human-like delays, so that I can prevent chain bans and maintain a stable sending capacity.
epics.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce account ban rate from baseline to under 5% across new campaigns.
epics.tocConstraintRef: Account banning restricts the maximum scale of daily outreach.
requirements: R1: Database schema to store Users, TG_Accounts, and proxy configurations.
R2: Shared API contract for managing accounts and proxies.
R3: Backend API to add accounts via OTP or .session upload and assign proxies.
R4: Core Telegram client integration for status monitoring and session authentication.
R5: Anti-fraud background worker enforcing 120-300s delays, typing imitation, and max 15-20 daily contacts.
R6: Background warm-up logic for accounts (subscribe, read posts, online presence).
R7: Frontend UI for operators to add accounts, attach proxies, and view statuses.

slices.title: Account & Proxy Data Schema
slices.jtbd: When implementing account storage for this epic, I want a structured schema for TG_Accounts and proxies, so that the backend can reliably track session state and limits.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a database migration script,
When it is executed,
Then the TG_Accounts and Users tables are created with proper foreign keys for proxy configuration.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Unreliable session storage prevents horizontal scaling.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce schema drift errors to 0% across environments.

slices.title: Account Management API Contract
slices.jtbd: When implementing account management for this epic, I want a defined OpenAPI contract, so that frontend and backend can be developed in parallel without integration mismatches.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the API requirements,
When the OpenAPI spec is generated,
Then it includes endpoints for adding accounts and querying status.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Frontend/backend integration mismatches delay feature delivery.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce API contract breaking changes during integration to 0.

slices.title: Core Telegram Auth & Proxy Client
slices.jtbd: When implementing Telegram client integration for this epic, I want to authenticate sessions via proxy, so that accounts can securely connect without leaking the host IP.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given valid OTP or .session data and a proxy,
When the service initializes the client,
Then a secure connection to Telegram is established via the proxy.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Direct IP exposure leads to immediate chain bans.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Increase successful proxy-authenticated Telegram sessions to 99.9%.

slices.title: Account Management Backend API
slices.jtbd: When implementing the backend for this epic, I want REST endpoints for account CRUD, so that the UI can securely add and assign proxies to sessions.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a valid API request to add an account,
When the backend processes it,
Then the account is saved in the DB and auth is triggered.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Lack of remote management prevents operators from scaling.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce API request latency for account status to <200ms.

slices.title: Anti-Fraud & Warm-up Worker
slices.jtbd: When implementing the background worker for this epic, I want to enforce random delays, typing status, and daily limits, so that account activity mimics human behavior.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given an active account,
When the worker runs,
Then it executes background reads and enforces a 120-300s delay before sending.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Rhythmic automated actions trigger Telegram spam filters.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Increase human-like behavior compliance to 100% of automated actions.

slices.title: Account Management UI
slices.jtbd: When implementing the frontend for this epic, I want a dashboard to add accounts and view statuses, so that operators can visually manage the account pool.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the Svelte admin panel,
When an operator navigates to accounts,
Then they can upload .session files and view active/banned status.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Manual database entry for accounts blocks operational speed.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce operator time to add a new account to <30 seconds.

epics.title: Outreach Campaign Management
epics.jtbd: When launching outreach, I want to import target bases and define spyntax templates, so that the system can automatically rotate accounts and send personalized messages without hitting spam limits.
epics.sixSigmaMetric: Increase daily successful message delivery volume by 500%.
epics.tocConstraintRef: Manual message sending and target tracking limits outreach volume.
requirements: R1: Database schema for Campaigns, Contact Bases, and message templates.
R2: Shared API contract for campaign configuration.
R3: Backend API to import CSV/TXT contact lists and configure templates.
R4: Spyntax parser and LLM-based first-sentence personalizer.
R5: Scheduling engine that handles account rotation and dynamic exponential timeouts.
R6: Svelte UI for campaign creation, target upload, and template testing.

slices.title: Campaign Data Schema
slices.jtbd: When implementing campaign storage for this epic, I want structured tables for campaigns and contacts, so that the backend can track target bases and progress.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the database,
When the schema migration is applied,
Then Campaigns and Leads tables are correctly structured.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Lack of structured campaign state prevents automated scheduling.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce schema validation errors during campaign creation to 0.

slices.title: Campaign API Contract
slices.jtbd: When implementing campaign logic for this epic, I want an OpenAPI specification, so that frontend and backend teams can integrate seamlessly.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the campaign requirements,
When the contract is published,
Then it includes CSV upload and template definition endpoints.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Integration friction between UI and backend delays launch.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Maintain 100% endpoint backward compatibility during initial dev.

slices.title: Campaign Management Backend API
slices.jtbd: When implementing the backend for this epic, I want endpoints to upload targets and define spyntax, so that the scheduler has data to process.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a valid CSV file of targets,
When uploaded to the API,
Then the contacts are parsed and stored in the database.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Manual lead entry limits campaign size.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce CSV parsing and insertion time to <2 seconds for 10k rows.

slices.title: Spyntax & Personalization Engine
slices.jtbd: When implementing message generation for this epic, I want a spyntax resolver and LLM personalizer, so that outgoing messages have unique signatures.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a template with {A|B},
When the engine processes it,
Then it outputs a randomly selected variant alongside an LLM-tailored intro.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Static message signatures lead to rapid spam filtering.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Increase unique message signature rate to >95% per campaign.

slices.title: Campaign Scheduler & Rotation Worker
slices.jtbd: When implementing the sender for this epic, I want a worker that rotates accounts and applies exponential timeouts, so that sending continues smoothly despite limits.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given an account hitting a limit,
When the scheduler runs,
Then it automatically switches to the next available account in the pool.
slices.tocConstraintRef: FLOOD_WAIT errors stall entire campaigns without rotation.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Decrease campaign stall time due to account limits to <1 minute.

slices.title: Campaign Management UI
slices.jtbd: When implementing the frontend for this epic, I want a campaign dashboard, so that marketers can visually upload contacts and test spyntax.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the Svelte dashboard,
When a user configures a campaign,
Then they can preview the randomized message output.
slices.tocConstraintRef: CLI-only campaign management prevents non-technical usage.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce campaign setup time for operators to <5 minutes.

epics.title: AI LLM Dialog Engine
epics.jtbd: When a target replies, I want an AI assistant to immediately intercept and continue the conversation, so that leads are automatically qualified and objections handled without manual effort.
epics.sixSigmaMetric: Increase automated lead qualification rate from 0% to >40%.
epics.tocConstraintRef: Manual response time to inbound messages causes lead drop-off.
requirements: R1: Database schema for Dialogs and Messages (history storage).
R2: Telegram event listener to capture incoming messages in real-time.
R3: Context management system to pass the last N messages to the LLM.
R4: LLM integration (OpenAI/Anthropic) with configurable system prompts.
R5: Stop-action engine to freeze AI on intent triggers (negative, ready to call) or after 5 replies.
R6: Shared API contract for AI behavior configuration.
R7: UI to configure system prompts and rules.

slices.title: Dialog & Message Data Schema
slices.jtbd: When implementing dialog storage for this epic, I want tables for conversations and messages, so that the AI has persistent context.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the database,
When the migration runs,
Then Dialogs and Messages tables are established with proper relations.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Loss of conversation history prevents AI from answering contextually.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Ensure 100% of processed incoming messages are persisted to DB.

slices.title: AI Configuration API Contract
slices.jtbd: When implementing AI configuration for this epic, I want an API specification, so that the frontend can safely configure system prompts.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the AI settings requirements,
When the spec is updated,
Then it includes endpoints for prompt and stop-trigger configuration.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Undefined AI config payloads cause frontend crashes.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce API shape mismatches in AI settings to 0.

slices.title: Telegram Inbound Event Listener
slices.jtbd: When implementing event capture for this epic, I want to listen for incoming Telegram messages, so that the system can trigger the AI engine.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given an active session,
When a target replies,
Then the event listener captures the message and stores it.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Missed inbound events result in dropped leads.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce event capture latency from Telegram to DB to <500ms.

slices.title: LLM Context & Prompt Integration
slices.jtbd: When implementing AI response generation for this epic, I want to feed context to the LLM, so that it generates relevant replies based on the system prompt.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a new inbound message,
When passed to the LLM service,
Then an appropriate response is generated using the last N messages.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Lack of contextual memory makes AI responses irrelevant.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Maintain average LLM response generation time under 3 seconds.

slices.title: AI Stop-Action & Intent Engine
slices.jtbd: When implementing AI controls for this epic, I want to detect specific intents or reply limits, so that the AI gracefully hands off to a human.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a conversation reaching 5 replies,
When the engine evaluates state,
Then the AI is frozen and status set to 'Awaiting Human'.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Unbounded AI loops frustrate targets and burn tokens.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Achieve 99% accuracy in freezing AI after max reply limit.

slices.title: AI Configuration UI
slices.jtbd: When implementing the frontend for this epic, I want a settings page for the AI, so that operators can adjust the system prompt and stop rules.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the admin dashboard,
When a user navigates to AI Settings,
Then they can edit the system prompt and save it to the backend.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Hardcoded AI prompts require developer time to update.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce prompt update deployment time from hours to instantaneous.

epics.title: Live Chat CRM
epics.jtbd: When AI hits its limits, I want a unified CRM interface to monitor and intercept all account dialogues, so that human operators can seamlessly close the deal.
epics.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce average human response time for intercepted chats to <2 minutes.
epics.tocConstraintRef: Scattered Telegram clients make it impossible for sales teams to monitor active leads.
requirements: R1: Shared API contract for the Live Chat interface.
R2: Backend CRM endpoints for unified inbox retrieval and real-time sync.
R3: Manual override endpoint to disable AI for a specific chat.
R4: Svelte CRM interface with color-coded dialogue states (AI active, Needs human, Completed, Spam).
R5: Real-time frontend updates for incoming messages.

slices.title: Live Chat API Contract
slices.jtbd: When implementing the CRM for this epic, I want a defined API contract, so that real-time messaging structures are agreed upon between frontend and backend.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the CRM requirements,
When the contract is published,
Then it details unified inbox and AI override endpoints.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Misalignment on message payload structures breaks real-time sync.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Ensure 100% adherence to API contract during CRM integration.

slices.title: CRM Backend & AI Override Logic
slices.jtbd: When implementing the CRM backend for this epic, I want unified inbox endpoints and an override toggle, so that operators can fetch chats and disable AI.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a human operator sends a message,
When the backend processes it,
Then the message is sent via Telegram and AI auto-reply is disabled for that chat.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Simultaneous human and AI replies confuse the target lead.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce AI override latency to <100ms upon human action.

slices.title: Live Chat Svelte UI
slices.jtbd: When implementing the CRM frontend for this epic, I want a color-coded unified inbox, so that operators can easily identify which chats need immediate human attention.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the live chat view,
When a dialog state changes to 'Needs human',
Then it is visually highlighted for the operator.
Given an operator types a message,
When sent,
Then it appears in the chat and triggers the override.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Operators missing high-intent leads due to UI clutter.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Decrease operator missed high-intent chats to <1%.
